meaning: A step-by-step set of instructions to solve a problem — like a recipe for a computer.
example: The algorithm sorted the list of names alphabetically in seconds.

meaning: Application Programming Interface — a way for two apps to talk to each other, like a waiter taking your order to the kitchen.
example: The weather app uses an API to fetch live temperature data.

meaning: The maximum amount of data that can travel through an internet connection at one time — like the width of a water pipe.
example: Streaming HD video requires high bandwidth.

meaning: A number system using only 0s and 1s — the basic language computers understand.
example: The letter 'A' is represented as 01000001 in binary.

meaning: A chain of digital records (blocks) that are linked and secured — like a tamper-proof public ledger.
example: Bitcoin transactions are stored on a blockchain.

meaning: The process of starting up a computer or device.
example: The laptop takes 15 seconds to boot after you press the power button.

meaning: A program used to explore the internet, like Chrome or Firefox.
example: She opened her browser and searched for the nearest hospital.

meaning: An error or flaw in a computer program that causes it to behave unexpectedly.
example: A bug in the app caused it to crash whenever a photo was uploaded.

meaning: A temporary storage area that saves frequently used data for quick access — like keeping a bookmark in a book.
example: Clearing the cache made the website load faster.

meaning: Storing and accessing data over the internet instead of your own computer's hard drive.
example: She saved her photos to the cloud so they would not be lost if her phone broke.

meaning: Instructions written in a programming language that tell a computer what to do.
example: The developer wrote code to build the login page.

meaning: Reducing the size of a file so it takes less space or transfers faster.
example: The image was compressed before being sent by email.

meaning: A small file stored in your browser that remembers your preferences and login details on websites.
example: The website used cookies to remember that you were already logged in.

meaning: Central Processing Unit — the main 'brain' of a computer that carries out instructions.
example: A faster CPU means the computer can run more programs at once.

meaning: The small arrow or blinking line on your screen that shows where you are pointing or typing.
example: She moved the cursor to the 'Submit' button and clicked.

meaning: The practice of protecting computers and data from attacks, theft, and damage.
example: Good cybersecurity practices include using strong passwords and two-factor authentication.

meaning: Facts and information stored or used by a computer — numbers, text, images, and more.
example: The hospital stores patient data electronically.

meaning: An organized collection of data stored so it can be easily found and used.
example: The school keeps all student records in a database.

meaning: The process of finding and fixing errors (bugs) in a computer program.
example: It took the programmer two hours to debug the payment system.

meaning: The setting or value a system uses automatically unless you change it.
example: The default language on the phone was English.

meaning: To release and make a software application available for use.
example: The team will deploy the new version of the app on Friday.

meaning: Any piece of hardware such as a phone, tablet, laptop, or printer.
example: You can access your files from any device connected to the internet.

meaning: Relating to data represented as numbers (0s and 1s) — the opposite of analogue.
example: A digital photograph is stored as data rather than on film.

meaning: The unique address of a website on the internet, like www.example.com.
example: She bought the domain name for her new online store.

meaning: Copying data from the internet or a server to your own device.
example: He downloaded the report as a PDF file.

meaning: A small program that tells the operating system how to communicate with a hardware device.
example: She installed a printer driver so the computer could recognise the new printer.

meaning: An unmanned flying vehicle controlled remotely or by a computer program.
example: Drones are used in agriculture to spray crops efficiently.

meaning: In technology, the collection of related software, hardware, and services that work together — like Apple's ecosystem of iPhone, Mac, and iCloud.
example: Many users stay within the Android ecosystem because all their apps and devices connect smoothly.

meaning: Scrambling data so that only authorised people with the right key can read it.
example: Your bank encrypts your personal details so hackers cannot steal them.

meaning: A mistake or problem in a program or system that causes it to work incorrectly.
example: An error message appeared saying the file could not be found.

meaning: A security system that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic — like a security guard for your computer.
example: The company's firewall blocked the suspicious email attachment.

meaning: Permanent software programmed into a device's hardware to control how it works.
example: Updating the router's firmware fixed the connection problems.

meaning: The structure or type in which information is stored, e.g. PDF, JPEG, or MP3.
example: The document was saved in PDF format so it could be opened on any device.

meaning: A pre-built structure or set of tools that developers use to build software more quickly.
example: The website was built using the React framework.

meaning: A unit of digital storage — roughly enough to store a thousand photos.
example: His phone had 128 gigabytes of storage.

meaning: Global Positioning System — technology that uses satellites to find your exact location on Earth.
example: She used GPS navigation to find the shortest route.

meaning: Visual elements such as images, animations, and designs displayed on a screen.
example: The new game had stunning 3D graphics.

meaning: A person who uses technical skills to access computer systems, sometimes without permission.
example: A hacker broke into the company's database and stole customer information.

meaning: The physical parts of a computer — the screen, keyboard, and processor you can touch.
example: The shop sells both hardware like laptops and software like antivirus programs.

meaning: The main or first page of a website that visitors see when they arrive.
example: The company's homepage displayed their latest products.

meaning: HyperText Markup Language — the standard language used to create the structure of web pages.
example: Every website you visit is built using HTML.

meaning: A device or platform that connects multiple devices or services together.
example: The smart home hub controlled all the lights and locks in the house.

meaning: A clickable word, phrase, or image on a web page that takes you to another page or site.
example: She clicked the hyperlink and was taken to the terms and conditions page.

meaning: A small image on a screen that represents an app, file, or function.
example: Tap the camera icon to open the photo app.

meaning: The basic physical and digital systems needed for technology to work — servers, cables, and data centres.
example: The government invested in digital infrastructure to bring fast internet to rural areas.

meaning: Data or information you put into a computer, e.g. typing, clicking, or speaking.
example: The form required input such as your name and email address.

meaning: The point where a user interacts with a computer — the buttons, menus, and screens.
example: The app has a simple interface that even beginners can use.

meaning: A global network connecting billions of computers and devices so they can share information.
example: She searched the internet for tips on learning English.

meaning: A unique number assigned to every device on a network — like a home address for computers.
example: Your IP address tells websites roughly where in the world you are located.

meaning: A hardware device with keys used to type text and commands into a computer.
example: She typed the password using the wireless keyboard.

meaning: A noticeable delay between an action and its result, especially in games or video calls.
example: There was so much lag during the video call that the audio kept cutting out.

meaning: A portable personal computer small enough to use on your lap.
example: He carried his laptop to the café to work on his presentation.

meaning: The time delay between sending and receiving data — lower latency means faster response.
example: Gamers prefer low-latency internet connections for smoother gameplay.

meaning: To enter your username and password to gain access to a system or website.
example: You need to log in with your email address to see your account details.

meaning: Malicious software designed to damage or gain unauthorised access to a computer system.
example: Her computer was infected with malware that slowed it down dramatically.

meaning: Random Access Memory — the computer's short-term memory used to run programs.
example: Opening too many tabs at once can use up all your computer's RAM.

meaning: A list of options on a screen from which a user can choose an action.
example: Click the File menu to save or open a document.

meaning: A device that connects your home or office to the internet through your phone or cable line.
example: She restarted the modem to fix the slow internet connection.

meaning: The screen of a desktop computer that displays images and text.
example: He plugged a second monitor into his laptop to have more screen space.

meaning: A small handheld device used to move a cursor on a computer screen and click on things.
example: She clicked the right mouse button to open the context menu.

meaning: A group of computers and devices connected together to share data and resources.
example: All computers in the office are connected to the same network.

meaning: An alert or message sent by an app or system to inform you of something new.
example: She received a notification that her order had been shipped.

meaning: Software whose source code is freely available for anyone to view, use, and improve.
example: Linux is a popular open source operating system.

meaning: The main software that manages a computer's hardware and allows other programs to run — like Windows or Android.
example: She updated her operating system to the latest version for better security.

meaning: Information or results produced by a computer, such as text on screen or a printed document.
example: The printer's output was a perfectly formatted report.

meaning: A software update designed to fix bugs or security problems.
example: The developer released a patch to fix the login error.

meaning: A secret combination of characters used to verify your identity and protect accounts.
example: Always use a strong password that includes numbers and special characters.

meaning: Any external device connected to a computer, such as a printer, scanner, or webcam.
example: She connected several peripherals to her desktop, including a printer and a microphone.

meaning: A scam where criminals pretend to be a trusted company to trick you into giving personal information.
example: He almost fell for a phishing email pretending to be from his bank.

meaning: The smallest unit of a digital image — thousands of tiny coloured dots make up the picture on your screen.
example: A higher pixel count means a sharper, clearer photo.

meaning: A base technology or service on which other applications are built and run.
example: YouTube is a platform for sharing and watching videos.

meaning: A small piece of software added to a program to give it extra features.
example: She installed a grammar-checking plugin for her word processor.

meaning: A small window that suddenly appears on screen, often showing an advertisement or alert.
example: The website was full of annoying pop-ups.

meaning: A physical connection point on a device (USB, HDMI) or a virtual channel number used in networking.
example: She plugged the charger into the USB port on the side of the laptop.

meaning: Your right to control who sees your personal information online.
example: Read the app's privacy settings to decide what data it can access.

meaning: The chip inside a computer that carries out instructions — the faster the processor, the quicker the computer.
example: The new smartphone has a faster processor for running complex apps.

meaning: A set of instructions that tells a computer how to perform a specific task.
example: Microsoft Word is a program used to write and edit documents.

meaning: A set of rules that governs how data is sent and received over a network.
example: HTTPS is a secure protocol that protects data sent between your browser and a website.

meaning: A square barcode that stores information and can be scanned by a phone camera.
example: Scan the QR code on the menu to see today's specials.

meaning: To restart a computer or device — often fixes minor problems.
example: The technician told him to reboot the router to restore the connection.

meaning: The number of pixels in an image or screen — higher resolution means a sharper picture.
example: The 4K monitor has four times the resolution of a standard HD screen.

meaning: A device that sends internet signals from your modem to your devices wirelessly or by cable.
example: Moving the router to the centre of the house improved the Wi-Fi signal.

meaning: An image of whatever is currently displayed on your screen.
example: She took a screenshot of the error message to send to technical support.

meaning: A tool that searches the internet and shows results matching your query — like Google or Bing.
example: He typed his question into the search engine and found the answer immediately.

meaning: A powerful computer that stores data and sends it to other computers when requested.
example: The website went down because the server was overloaded with visitors.

meaning: Options that allow you to customise how a device or application works.
example: Go to Settings to change your notification preferences.

meaning: A quicker way to perform an action, often using a key combination like Ctrl+C to copy.
example: Use the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Z to undo your last action.

meaning: The strength of a wireless connection — shown as bars on your phone.
example: There was no phone signal deep inside the tunnel.

meaning: An electronic device that connects to the internet and can be controlled remotely.
example: Smart devices like thermostats can be controlled from your phone.

meaning: Programs and applications that run on a computer — the instructions, not the physical parts.
example: The company uses accounting software to manage its finances.

meaning: Unwanted or irrelevant messages sent in bulk, especially by email.
example: He set up a filter to send spam emails directly to the trash folder.

meaning: Watching or listening to media over the internet without downloading it first.
example: She spent the evening streaming movies on her tablet.

meaning: Short for synchronise — making data identical across two or more devices automatically.
example: Her contacts synced automatically between her phone and laptop.

meaning: A group of hardware and software components working together to perform tasks.
example: The hospital's information system keeps track of all patient appointments.

meaning: A portable touchscreen computer, larger than a phone but smaller than a laptop.
example: The children used tablets to do their homework online.

meaning: A pre-designed document or layout that you can fill in and customise.
example: She used a CV template to create her job application.

meaning: A text-based interface for typing commands directly to a computer's operating system.
example: The developer used the terminal to run a script that updated the server.

meaning: A display that responds to touch, allowing you to control the device with your fingers.
example: Modern smartphones use a touchscreen instead of physical buttons.

meaning: A new version of software that adds features or fixes problems.
example: Install the latest update to keep your phone secure.

meaning: Sending data from your device to the internet or a server.
example: She uploaded her assignment to the college portal before midnight.

meaning: Uniform Resource Locator — the web address you type to visit a website, e.g. https://www.example.com.
example: Copy the URL from the address bar and send it to your friend.

meaning: Universal Serial Bus — a standard connector used to plug in devices like flash drives and chargers.
example: She transferred the photos using a USB cable.

meaning: Any person who interacts with a computer, app, or digital service.
example: The app has over one million active users worldwide.

meaning: A specific release of a software product — newer versions usually have more features.
example: Make sure you are using the latest version of the browser.

meaning: Malicious software that copies itself and spreads to other devices, causing damage.
example: An antivirus program detected and removed the virus before it could steal any data.

meaning: Virtual Private Network — a service that hides your internet activity and location for privacy and security.
example: She used a VPN to safely connect to public Wi-Fi at the airport.

meaning: A camera built into or attached to a computer used for video calls.
example: He turned on the webcam for his online interview.

meaning: A collection of pages on the internet connected under one address.
example: The school created a website to share news with parents.

meaning: A wireless technology that allows devices to connect to the internet without cables.
example: The hotel provided free Wi-Fi in all rooms.

meaning: A rectangular area on your screen where a program or file is displayed.
example: She opened a new window to compare two documents side by side.

meaning: Transmitting data without physical cables, using radio waves or infrared signals.
example: He connected his wireless headphones to the laptop via Bluetooth.

meaning: To enlarge or reduce what is displayed on screen; also a popular video-calling application.
example: She zoomed in on the map to find the exact street.

meaning: Short for application — a software program designed for a specific purpose on a phone or computer.
example: He downloaded a meditation app to help him sleep better.

meaning: Technology that allows machines to perform tasks that normally require human intelligence, like understanding speech or recognising images.
example: The customer service chatbot is powered by artificial intelligence.

meaning: Using technology to perform repetitive tasks with little or no human involvement.
example: Automation in the factory reduced production time by 40%.

meaning: A copy of your data stored separately so it can be restored if the original is lost.
example: She made a backup of all her files before updating the operating system.

meaning: A wireless technology for short-range connections between devices, like phones and headphones.
example: He connected his phone to the car speaker via Bluetooth.

meaning: A computer program that simulates human conversation and answers questions automatically.
example: The bank's website has a chatbot that answers basic queries 24/7.

meaning: When a program or system suddenly stops working and closes unexpectedly.
example: The app crashed and she lost the paragraph she had just written.

meaning: The rhythmic flashing of the text cursor that shows where your next typed character will appear.
example: She watched the cursor blink, waiting for inspiration to write.

meaning: A screen showing key information and controls for a system at a glance — like the gauges in a car.
example: The analytics dashboard showed how many people visited the website each day.

meaning: Buying and selling products or services over the internet.
example: Her e-commerce business sells handmade jewellery worldwide.

meaning: Electronic mail — messages sent and received instantly over the internet.
example: He sent the contract to the client by email.

meaning: A named collection of data stored on a device — documents, photos, or videos are all files.
example: She saved the report as a PDF file.

meaning: A container used to organise files on a computer — like a physical folder in a filing cabinet.
example: He created a folder called 'Projects 2025' to organise his work.

meaning: A small, temporary error in a system that causes unexpected behaviour.
example: There was a glitch in the app that made the screen flicker.

meaning: The storage device inside a computer where all files, programs, and the operating system are saved.
example: His hard drive was nearly full, so he deleted old videos to free up space.

meaning: A location where Wi-Fi is available, or a feature that lets your phone share its mobile internet with other devices.
example: She used her phone as a hotspot when the café Wi-Fi was too slow.

meaning: To set up a program or app so it is ready to use on your device.
example: He installed an antivirus program to protect his laptop.

meaning: The visual layout of a program that you interact with — buttons, icons, menus, and screens.
example: The new interface made it much easier to find settings.

meaning: A type of AI where computers learn from data to improve their performance without being directly programmed.
example: Machine learning helps email systems detect spam automatically.

meaning: A unit of digital data equal to roughly one million bytes — used to measure file sizes.
example: The photo was 4 megabytes in size.

meaning: A tiny electronic circuit embedded in a small flat piece of silicon, found in nearly all electronic devices.
example: Modern microchips are smaller than a fingernail but contain billions of tiny components.

meaning: Not connected to the internet — working only with what is stored locally on your device.
example: You can use the document editor offline and it will sync when you reconnect.

meaning: Connected to and accessible through the internet.
example: The library offers online access to thousands of books and journals.

meaning: Connecting two Bluetooth devices to each other so they can communicate.
example: She started pairing her new wireless earbuds with her phone.

meaning: An advertisement that opens in a new window or appears suddenly over a webpage.
example: He installed an ad blocker to stop pop-up ads interrupting his reading.

meaning: In technology, apps or tools designed to help you work more efficiently.
example: She used productivity apps to manage her tasks and schedule.

meaning: Accessing or controlling a computer or system from a different location over the internet.
example: The IT team fixed the problem using remote access to his computer.

meaning: A new version of a program released to add features, improve performance, or fix security issues.
example: A software update fixed the bug that was causing crashes.

meaning: The space available on a device to keep files, photos, apps, and other data.
example: Her phone ran out of storage, so she deleted some videos.

meaning: A payment model where you pay regularly (monthly or yearly) to use a service or app.
example: She paid a monthly subscription for the music streaming service.

meaning: Help provided by a company to fix problems with their products or services.
example: He contacted technical support when the printer stopped working.

meaning: A flat surface on a laptop used to move the cursor by sliding your finger across it.
example: She used the touchpad instead of a mouse to navigate the document.

meaning: A security method requiring two forms of verification — usually a password plus a code sent to your phone.
example: Enable two-factor authentication to make your account much harder to hack.

meaning: To remove a program or app from a device.
example: He uninstalled the game to free up space on his phone.

meaning: The name you use to identify yourself when logging into a website or app.
example: Her username was her first name followed by her birth year.

meaning: Existing in digital form rather than physically — simulated by a computer.
example: The virtual meeting replaced the need to travel to the office.

meaning: An AI-powered tool that responds to spoken commands — like Siri, Alexa, or Google Assistant.
example: She asked her voice assistant to set a reminder for her 3 pm meeting.

meaning: A single document on the internet that can contain text, images, and links.
example: The article was published on a dedicated web page.

meaning: A small interactive element on an app or webpage that displays information at a glance.
example: She added a weather widget to her phone's home screen.

meaning: Connected using a physical cable rather than wirelessly.
example: A wired connection is usually faster and more reliable than Wi-Fi.
